---
title: "Jian Zou"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false
---

<section class="hero">
<div class="hero-body">
<div class="eyebrow">Biomedical Data Science</div>
<h1 class="display">Statistical learning methods for <em>clinical</em> and high-dimensional <em>omics</em> data.</h1>
<p class="lede">I am Jian Zou (邹鉴), an Associate Professor at the <a href="https://gwgl.cqmu.edu.cn/info/1215/6841.htm">Department of Statistics, School of Public Health</a>, Chongqing Medical University. </p>
<p>Prior to CQMU, I completed my Ph.D. in Biostatistics at the <a href="https://www.publichealth.pitt.edu/">School of Public Health</a>, University of Pittsburgh in 2023, under the supervision of <a href="http://www.pitt.edu/~ctseng/">Dr. George C. Tseng</a>, and co-advised by <a href="https://www.pharmacology.us/Faculty/SteffiOesterreich">Dr. Steffi Oesterreich</a> and <a href="https://www.pharmacology.us/Faculty/AdrianLee">Dr. Adrian Lee</a> at the UPMC Hillman Cancer Center. I earned an M.S. in Biostatistics at the <a href="https://www.mailman.columbia.edu/">Mailman School of Public Health</a>, Columbia University in 2019, and dual B.S. degrees in Biotechnology and Applied Chemistry from the <a href="http://bio.ccnu.edu.cn/">School of Life Science</a>, Central China Normal University in 2017.</p>
<p>My CV is available <a href="downloads/CV_JianZou.pdf">here</a> (<a href="downloads/CV_JianZou_cn.pdf">Chinese version</a>).</p>
</div>
<aside class="side">
<div class="side-label">Recent</div>
<div class="side-item"><b><a href="https://doi.org/10.59717/j.xinn-oncol.2026.100012">Redefining the tumor digital twin through cross-domain manifold mapping.</a></b><i>TIO · 2026</i></div>
<div class="side-item"><b><a href="https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btae696">Mutual information for multi-class biomarker detection.</a></b><i>Bioinformatics · 2024</i></div>
<div class="side-item"><b><a href="https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1011754">Systems approach to cancer-model selection (CASCAM).</a></b><i>PLoS Comput Biol · 2024</i></div>
<div class="side-item"><b><a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s43018-025-00969-4">p95HER2 drives an immunosuppressive program in HER2+ breast cancer.</a></b><i>Nature Cancer · 2025</i></div>
</aside>
</section>
